title: Packing for a Trip: Hearing Plurals
learningGoal: The learner will be able to distinguish between singular and plural nouns in fast-paced spoken English, particularly identifying whether an object had an indefinite article (A/An) or a plural suffix sound (S/Z/IZ).
instructions: Listen to a mother (Kavita) and her son (Rahul) packing for a trip. They are checking their list. Listen very closely to whether they say 'A bag' (one) or 'Bags' (many). Answer the 8 questions below.

contentItems.text: The audio is at native speed. Use the Slow track if you have trouble hearing the 'S' or 'ES' endings.

listeningGoal: Accurately parse auditory cues to count items (singular vs plural) based on the presence of articles and plural suffix sounds.
audioScenario: Kavita and her son Rahul are in their living room, packing for a weekend trip to Delhi. They are going through a checklist.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Rahul, are we ready for the trip to Delhi?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: राहुल, आर वी रेडी फॉर द ट्रिप टू दिल्ली?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: राहुल, क्या हम दिल्ली यात्रा के लिए तैयार हैं?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Yes, Mom. I have a bag.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: येस, मॉम। आई हैव अ बैग।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: हाँ, माँ। मेरे पास एक थैला है।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Only a bag? We need two bags.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: ओनली अ बैग? वी नीड टू बैग्स।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: केवल एक थैला? हमें दो थैलों की आवश्यकता है।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Okay, I have two bags. The bags are heavy.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: ओके, आई हैव टू बैग्स। द बैग्स आर हेवी।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: ठीक है, मेरे पास दो थैले हैं। थैले भारी हैं।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: No, I packed three apples and an orange.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: नो, आई पैक्ड थ्री ऐपल्स एंड ऐन ऑरेंज।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: नहीं, मैंने तीन सेब और एक संतरा रखा।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Good. Do we need an umbrella?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: गुड। डू वी नीड ऐन अम्ब्रेला?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: अच्छा। क्या हमें एक छाते की आवश्यकता है?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Yes, it is raining. I packed two umbrellas.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: येस, इट इज़ रेनिंग। आई पैक्ड टू अम्ब्रेलाज़।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: हाँ, बारिश हो रही है। मैंने दो छाते रखे।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: These boxes? They are my books.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: दीज़ बॉक्सेस? दे आर माई बुक्स।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: ये डिब्बे? वे मेरी किताबें हैं।

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: Okay. The buses are coming. Let's go.
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: ओके। द बसेस आर कमिंग। लेट्स गो।
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: ठीक है। बसें आ रही हैं। चलो चलते हैं।

audioTranscriptFull:
Kavita: Rahul, are we ready for the trip to Delhi?
Rahul: Yes, Mom. I have a bag.
Kavita: Only a bag? We need two bags.
Rahul: Okay, I have two bags. The bags are heavy.
Kavita: Did you pack an apple?
Rahul: No, I packed three apples and an orange.
Kavita: Good. Do we need an umbrella?
Rahul: Yes, it is raining. I packed two umbrellas.
Kavita: What is in those boxes?
Rahul: These boxes? They are my books.
Kavita: Okay. The buses are coming. Let's go.

questionSet.questionText: Where are Kavita and Rahul going?
Initially, how many bags did Rahul say he had: 'A bag' or 'Two bags'?
How many APPLES did Rahul pack?
According to the audio, what weather is happening?
What exact word does Kavita use for the plural of box? 'Boxs' or 'Boxes'?
What vehicle are they waiting for at the end of the tape?

scoringRules.partialCreditRules: No partial credit for Q4. Plural recognition is binary.

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You were able to hear when a noun had an article (meaning singular) and when it had an S/ES sound (meaning plural) at native speed.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Slow the audio down. Listen to how Rahul says 'an orange', but then says 'three apples'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good ear! You caught most of it. But remember, the audio specifically said 'Buses' (plural) at the end, not 'A bus'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Kavita: Rahul, are we ready for the trip to Delhi? ... (matches full transcript)

hintText: Plurals ending in '-es' add a whole new syllable. 'Boxes' has two beats, while 'Box' has one beat.
commonMistakes: Not hearing the 'es' syllable in boxes.
